title: Parties must sign Lok Adalat award to make it binding, lawyer's signature not enough: Karnataka HC
description: The Karnataka High Court recently set aside a Lok Adalat award on finding that it was not signed by one side of the parties to the dispute, but rather only by their lawyer [Shaila & Ors v ICICI Lombard GIC Ltd & Ors].Justice M Nagaprasanna was dealing with a case where a woman and her son (petitioners) challenged the validity of a Lok Adalat award that reduced the compensation payable to them for a motor accident. The petitioners argued that the Lok Adalat award was passed without their
